title: E-payment transactions’ Value Rises to N664.21trn In 2023
description: The value of electronic payment (E-payment) transactions grew by 66 percent to N664.21 trillion in 2023 from N387 trillion in 2022. This reflects Nigerians’ increased adoption of electronic payment ch
html:
The value of electronic payment (E-payment) transactions grew by 66 percent to N664.21 trillion in 2023 from N387 trillion in 2022.

This reflects Nigerians’ increased adoption of electronic payment channels, driven by cash scarcity and increased financial inclusion.

Following the same trend, the volume of electronic payment transactions increased by 240 percent to 17.34 billion from 5.1 billion in 2022.

According to Nigerian Interbank Settlement System, NIBSS, e-payment data for 2023, the highest volume of transactions (1.76 billion) was recorded in December  reflecting increased spending due to the festive season.

The lowest volume was recorded in March with 978.7 million transactions.

Furthermore, the highest value of transactions (N79 trillion) was recorded in December 2023 while the lowest was recorded in February 2022 with N40.9 trillion transactions.

According to Enhancing Financial Innovation and Access, EFiNA, 2023 Access to Finance Survey Report, 45 percent of Nigerians used Digital Financial Services in the past 12 months, up from 34 percent in 2020.

“Financial inclusion continues to grow, even as formal financial inclusion grew from 56 percent (2020) to 64 percent (2023).
